I may suggest one thing. 

A lot of people may be using it to just try it. Or a competitor might generate a lot of licenses and not use it.

Maybe you want to put a time bar for each license generated to make it not quick and instant.

author: Matej
msg:
#amaquestion 

The Unity application urgently needs a better UX because now the new user does not understand it at all. There are no notes anywhere within the app, no explanations of individual functions and displays, no instructions with basic concepts and it could go on and on. ALL OF THIS MUST BE IN THE APP - NOT SOMEWHERE ELSEWHERE AND EXTERNAL MEDIA. In short, the UX is extremely poor and repels rather than attracts new users. The new user cannot even see or look at the rules of how the UP rewards work, let alone how and where the rewards are paid - AGAIN WITHIN THE APP, NOT SOMEWHERE ELSEWHERE. So, my question is: Is this being worked on and how far is the completion of a suitable UX because Unity needs a lot of users? The app as it is now will not be used by an ordinary uneducated user because it is not at all clear how to use it?

It seems that we'll never leave the alpha phase for any of the tasks.
